channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Volodímir Zelenski comienza a distanciarse del gobierno de Trump
item.description: Con las conversaciones de paz congeladas y Ucrania ahora más autosuficiente, el presidente Volodímir Zelenski parece alejarse de EE. UU.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker, Constant Méheut and Maria Varenikova
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El presidente de Ucrania, Volodímir Zelenski, en Ereván, Armenia, la semana pasada. Zelenski ha criticado públicamente a Estados Unidos de formas que habrían sido impensables el año pasado.

item.title: La azotea en la que los Beatles tocaron juntos por última vez será un museo
item.description: El inmueble del número 3 de la calle Savile Row, donde el cuarteto también grabó el álbum “Let It Be” se abrirá al público el año que viene.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall

media:content.media:description: Los Beatles durante su última presentación en el techo del número 3 de la calle Savile Row, en Londres, en 1969.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘remonstrate’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en un artículo de NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Live Updates: Britain’s Starmer Faces Rebellion in His Party
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer was expected to meet with cabinet members on Tuesday. Dozens of Labour Party lawmakers urged him to step down after heavy losses in local elections.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Deadly Gang Feud Left Bystander Paralyzed in Brooklyn
item.description: The police said 15 people were indicted after a series of shootings across Brooklyn that were linked to gang disputes. At least six people were injured and another was fatally shot.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer

media:content.media:description: Brooklyn gangs targeted rivals in a series of shootings, like this one at the Flatbush Gardens housing complex, according to law enforcement officials.

item.title: A Text System Sent Safety Alerts to Private Schools. Now, It’s Silent.
item.description: New York City’s emergency management agency, which ran the alert network, said it needed to free up personnel to help translate messages for the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag and Dana Rubinstein

media:content.media:credit: Marcus Yam for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: At the Horace Mann School, a private school in the Bronx, the security team works closely with the local police precinct and monitors social media and other sites to learn about potential safety concerns.

item.title: A Reporter Shares His Secrets to Covering the Cannes Film Festival
item.description: Kyle Buchanan, who has attended the annual launchpad for Oscar contenders in France since 2013, subsists on Red Bull and around four hours of sleep.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:description: The red carpet at the 2025 Cannes Film Festival. Many movies screened during the festival go on to earn Academy Award nominations.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 12, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Stephen Colbert Hosts 4 of His ‘Best Television Friends’
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel, Jimmy Fallon, John Oliver and Seth Meyers, co-hosts of the Strike Force Five podcast along with Colbert, joined as guests on what is likely their last appearance together on the “Late Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Two Critically Injured After Gunman Fires Into Traffic in Cambridge
item.description: As people fled their cars along the busy riverfront road, a state trooper and an armed civilian ran toward the gunman and exchanged fire with him.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young

media:content.media:description: Police officers respond to a man who had been seen shooting a weapon along Memorial Drive in Cambridge, Massachusetts, on Monday.

item.title: Word of the Day: remonstrate
item.description: This word has appeared in one article on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Instructure Strikes Deal for Hackers for Return of Canvas Data
item.description: Instructure, which provides Canvas software to thousands of schools and universities around the world, did not say what it had given the hackers in exchange for the stolen data.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman

media:content.media:credit: Alessandro Penso for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Hackers stole data from Canvas, an education platform used by thousands of schools and universities around the world.

item.title: Pedro Sánchez Is Showing How to Stand Up to Trump, and So Much More
item.description: Pedro Sánchez has established an alternative governing philosophy to Trumpism.
item.dc:creator: Omar G. Encarnación

item.title: Robert Kennedy Jr. continúa su cruzada para investigar las vacunas
item.description: Kennedy lidera una intensa campaña para que científicos, y los contratistas federales de datos, analicen su teoría de que las vacunas impulsan una epidemia de enfermedades crónicas.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: How to Eat Through Our List of the Best Restaurants in New York City
item.description: Don’t have the time (or money) to try all 100? Here are a dozen bite-size bucket lists to inspire you.
item.dc:creator: Nikita Richardson, Becky Hughes, Luke Fortney, Mahira Rivers, Ryan Sutton, Eric Asimov, Ligaya Mishan and Rozalina Burkova

item.title: Is France’s Centrist Leader Trying to Weatherproof It From the Far Right?
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron has appointed a string of allies who will remain in critical state positions after next year’s presidential election, and who could thwart a far-right successor.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler

media:content.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France speaking in Yerevan, Armenia, last week. He cannot run for re-election in France’s next presidential election.

item.title: Putin dijo que la guerra ‘está llegando a su fin’, pero no cede en sus términos
item.description: El dirigente ruso camina por la cuerda floja: reconoce el descontento de la opinión pública, pero no ofrece indicios de que pueda renunciar a sus exigencias.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne

media:content.media:credit: Foto de consorcio por Alexander Nemenov
media:content.media:description: El presidente Vladimir Putin durante la celebración, el sábado en Moscú, de la victoria soviética sobre la Alemania nazi.

item.title: Xi Is Poised to Press Trump on Arms Sales to Taiwan
item.description: Beijing has called Taiwan the “core of China’s core interests.” Xi Jinping is likely to focus on getting President Trump to slow approval of more weapons for the self-governing island.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

media:content.media:description: A high mobility artillery rocket system, or HIMARS, displayed during a military exercise in Taichung, Taiwan, last year.

item.title: China Seeks A.I. Independence, Weakening Trump’s Leverage
item.description: Before this week’s U.S.-Chinese summit, Beijing reached a milestone in its quest for technological self-sufficiency.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin

media:content.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: DeepSeek said for the first time that its new model had been optimized to run on chips made by the Chinese tech giant Huawei.

item.title: Altar Boy Who Called Out Sexual Abuse in the Philippines Is Now a Lawyer for Victims
item.description: Michal Gatchalian dared to speak out against sexual abuse by a priest in the devoutly Catholic Philippines. He’s now a lawyer helping other victims.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Aie Balagtas See and Jes Aznar

media:content.media:description: Michal Gatchalian last year in front of a makeshift altar he had setup at his suburban village’s clubhouse. He no longer goes to the church where he was an altar boy.

item.title: China Increasingly Views Trump’s America as an Empire in Decline
item.description: For decades, many Chinese viewed the United States with a mix of admiration, envy and resentment. President Trump’s volatile second term shattered that image.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan

media:content.media:description: The port at Tianjin, China. In response to President Trump’s tariffs, China has become more assertive on the global stage.

item.title: Quote of the Day: What Asia Fears Most On Summit
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, May 12, 2026.

item.title: Judge Again Delays Guantánamo’s First Death-Penalty Terror Trial
item.description: The 2000 terrorism case has been going on for so long that the parents of fallen sailors and shipmates who survived the attack have died.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: The U.S.S. Cole bombing case is on track to become the first capital trial at Guantánamo Bay. Jury selection was again delayed.

item.title: At Frieze New York and Beyond, Indigenous Artists Are in the Spotlight
item.description: With works showing in New York, Venice and elsewhere, Sara Flores, Suzanne Kite and Seba Calfuqueo are exploding past notions of what, exactly, Indigenous art is.
item.dc:creator: Nazanin Lankarani

item.title: I Have Some Questions for the Democrats Who Want to Run California
item.description: How will the next governor make housing more affordable? The top five Democratic candidates in California discuss this issue at a forum moderated by Ezra Klein.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Justice Dept. Subpoenas Wall Street Journal in Leak Investigation
item.description: Critics raised concerns over press freedom after The Journal disclosed receiving subpoenas related to an article on deliberations on the risks of military action in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:description: The Wall Street Journal headquarters in New York. The inquiry into the origins of The Journal’s reporting is one of multiple leak investigations being conducted by the U.S. attorney’s office in the Eastern District of Virginia, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Lista de países de los pasajeros del crucero con un brote de hantavirus
item.description: Casi dos decenas de países están repatriando a ciudadanos que iban a bordo del MV Hondius, donde murieron tres pasajeros en un brote de hantavirus.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young

media:content.media:description: Ciudadanos británicos repatriados del MV Hondius llegando a un hospital del noroeste de Inglaterra el domingo.

item.title: Kari Lake, Who Led Effort to Shutter Voice of America, Is Named Ambassador to Jamaica
item.description: Ms. Lake tried and failed to fire hundreds of Voice of America journalists and to cut funding for other federally funded news groups like Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: United States Agency for Global Media

media:content.media:credit: Maansi Srivastava for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kari Lake speaking at the annual CPAC event in Oxon Hill, Md., last year.

item.title: Bezos Family Gives $100 Million for Preschool Education in New York
item.description: Free child care is one of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s top goals. Now the Bezos Foundation is giving Robin Hood, a favorite philanthropy of wealthy New Yorkers, big money for early childhood education.
item.dc:creator: Eliza Shapiro
item.category: Mayor's Fund to Advance New York

media:content.media:credit: Dolly Faibyshev for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Robin Hood’s chief executive, Richard R. Buery, Jr., said he is encouraged that Mayor Zohran Mamdani and philanthropists agree on funding child care.

item.title: Corrections: May 12, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Tuesday, May 12, 2026.

item.title: San Jose’s Approach to Homelessness
item.description: San Jose’s mayor, Matt Mahan, tells the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein why California’s reliance on expensive, slow-moving affordable-housing projects has eroded public trust, and argues for a “radically pragmatic” shift toward interim housing and stricter encampment enforcement.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Arcadia Mayor Eileen Wang Will Plead Guilty to Working as Chinese Agent
item.description: Eileen Wang, who resigned Monday as the mayor of Arcadia, Calif., published propaganda on a purported news site under direction from Chinese officials, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: Eileen Wang at the Asian Hall of Fame induction ceremony in Los Angeles in 2023.

item.title: Katie Porter on the California Forever Project
item.description: How would Katie Porter, a Democratic candidate for California governor, navigate the billionaire-backed California Forever project if elected? She discusses her approach with the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein during a live forum.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Tom Steyer’s Plan to Fix Modular Housing
item.description: Why has modular housing failed to revolutionize the industry, despite decades of hype? Tom Steyer tells the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein why modular housing start-ups have failed and how California can use its scale to finally make home construction more affordable.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Inside the Hantavirus Quarantine Unit in Nebraska Where 15 Americans Wait
item.description: The National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska is now the temporary home for many of the Americans who had been passengers on the MV Hondius.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao

media:content.media:description: The Davis Global Center, at the University of Nebraska Medical Center campus in Omaha, houses the National Quarantine Unit.

item.title: Democrats Can’t Let This Antisemitic Sex Therapist Win Her Runoff
item.description: Maureen Galindo’s rise demonstrates how even justified rage and despair can curdle into hateful paranoia.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by Aleksey Kondratyev for The New York Times

item.title: 1-Year-Old Boy Killed in Bronx Fire
item.description: Two other children, 6-year-old twins, were critically injured in the fire at an apartment building in the Fordham neighborhood, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman and Wesley Parnell

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Monday’s fire on Bainbridge Avenue in Fordham was the latest in a string of fatal fires in the Bronx.

item.title: Supreme Court Clears Path for Alabama to Use New Voting Map
item.description: A majority of the justices sided with Alabama in a move that could speed up efforts to put in place a congressional district map that would eliminate a majority-Black district.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane

item.title: Student New Quiz: National Debt, Teen Takeovers, Sea Lion
item.description: Have you been paying attention to current events recently? See how well you can do on this week’s news quiz for students.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Engle

item.title: Dua Lipa Sues Samsung Over Use of Her Image on TV Packaging
item.description: Dua Lipa sued Samsung for $15 million, accusing the South Korean electronics giant of using her image on TV packaging without her permission.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A person loading a boxed Samsung TV into a car in New York in November.

item.title: Senate G.O.P. Stands by Security Money for Trump’s Ballroom
item.description: Democrats are trying to stoke public opposition to the project as they take aim at legislation to provide nearly $72 billion for the administration’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.category: White House East Wing and Ballroom (Washington, DC)

media:content.media:description: Construction continued on the East Wing last month.

item.title: Microsoft’s C.E.O. Intervened When OpenAI Fired Sam Altman, Musk’s Lawyer Claims
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawyer argued that Microsoft’s Satya Nadella played a role in getting Mr. Altman his job back at OpenAI when he was briefly fired in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Satya Nadella, Microsoft’s chief executive, led his company’s investments into OpenAI.

item.title: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax
item.description: Also, Kennedy is pushing a vast inquiry into vaccines. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: A gas station in Pasadena, Calif. today.

item.title: Former Hostage Calls on All of Israel’s Government to Resign
item.description: Rom Braslavski, who said he had endured starvation, torture and sexual abuse during two-year captivity, demanded that members “take responsibility and get out of our lives.”
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: Rom Braslavski, in glasses, with three other former hostages during a ceremony in Tel Aviv in January marking the return of Israel’s last remaining hostage.

item.title: ¿Eres frágil?
item.description: Te decimos cómo se diagnostica la fragilidad, en qué se diferencia del envejecimiento normal, qué te pone en riesgo de volverte frágil y qué hacer al respecto.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith

item.title: Trump Calls Iran’s Counterproposal a ‘Piece of Garbage’
item.description: President Trump told reporters the Iran cease-fire was “on life support” on Monday after he rejected Iran’s counterproposal for a peace deal this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:credit: Foad Ashtari/SOPA Images, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Esmail Baghaei, the spokesman for Iran’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs, in Tehran last month.

item.title: The Iran War’s Alarming Ripple Effects
item.description: The downstream effects of the Iran war are only beginning. On “The Opinions,” David Wallace-Wells explains how disruptions tied to fertilizer, condoms and other global goods could contribute to famine, disease and rising costs worldwide.

item.title: Former Top Zelensky Aide Is Accused in Corruption Inquiry
item.description: Ukrainian news outlets named the suspect as Andriy Yermak, who resigned last year amid a sweeping scandal.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:description: Andriy Yermak resigned in November after law enforcement agencies searched his apartment as part of a corruption investigation, but he was not charged.

item.title: El crucero afectado por un brote de hantavirus deja las islas Canarias
item.description: Los últimos evacuados desembarcaron en las islas Canarias, y una tripulación más reducida se dirige ahora a Rotterdam, donde se desinfectará el barco, dijeron las autoridades.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:description: Tres personas que iban a bordo del MV Hondius han muerto desde que zarpó de Argentina en abril.

item.title: How Democratic Divisions Are Playing Out in NYC’s Primary Races
item.description: Establishment and left-wing leaders are clashing over House seats scattered across the city.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Democrats are clashing over a number of House seats across New York City.
